summary: A solid soundboard capture with crisp highs and a strong second‑set climax – Shakedown opens the night, while Corrina, The Other One, Morning Dew and the post‑Space segment shine.
review: The SBD of this 1992 stadium show is generally clean and well‑balanced, though a few listeners note occasional muddiness and pitch quirks. Musically the band delivers an energetic first set that stumbles on the opening Shakedown but recovers spectacularly after Space, with Corrina, The Other One, Ship of Fools and a soaring Morning Dew anchoring the second half.

ai_rating.rationale: Average user star rating (≈3.3) across 21 reviews, with many praising the performance and soundboard quality but a notable number of comments about mix issues, muffled sections, and weaker moments.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Most reviewers describe the tape as clean, crisp and well‑balanced; some note occasional muddiness, a blip at the start of "Don't Ease", pitch/speed anomalies, and isolated mix problems.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is described as chaotic but solid, with the opening Shakedown receiving mixed reactions. After Space the second set lifts dramatically, delivering tight jams and memorable segues that leave most fans rating the latter half highly.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Frequently praised for fiery, on‑fire guitar work and powerful solos; a few critics note his playing sounded rushed or inconsistent in later years.
band_member_comments.Phil: Consistently highlighted for a deep, fat bass presence and inspiring moments (especially the opening lick on The Other One).
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince is often singled out as a highlight of this era – praised for tasteful playing and not being too loud, though some feel his keyboards sit forward in the mix or that he was over‑present.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill/Mickey receive positive notes for inventive drums, high‑life style riffs, and even a memorable train‑horn moment; overall drum work is called "interesting" and energizing.
